title: caucalis scandix-nodosa
intro: Caucalis scandix-nodosa, also known as Wild Carrot or Scotch Carrot, is a flowering plant that belongs to the Apiaceae family and is native to Europe, Western Asia, and North Africa. Traditionally, Caucalis scandix-nodosa has been used for its medicinal properties, particularly in the treatment of digestive issues, such as indigestion and bloating, due to its carminative and anti-inflammatory effects. The plant is also believed to have health benefits, including the ability to reduce fever, alleviate menstrual cramps, and support the immune system. In herbal preparations, the roots and leaves of Caucalis scandix-nodosa are often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be consumed to harness its medicinal properties.
